input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to create an AppShell.xaml for a .NET MAUI app with a specific navigation structure. Let me provide the code directly since this is a straightforward request that doesn't require codebase exploration.

Structure needed:
1. Flyout with two sections:
   - "Dashboard" (single page)
   - "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and "Archived")
2. Settings page accessible from the flyout
3. Route registration for ProductDetailsPage

Let me create the XAML and the code-behind.

data.content:
Here's the full setup:

**AppShell.xaml**

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<Shell x:Class="MyApp.AppShell"
       xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
       xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
       xmlns:views="clr-namespace:MyApp.Views"
       FlyoutBehavior="Flyout"
       Title="MyApp">

    <!-- Dashboard — standalone flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="icon_dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:DashboardPage}" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products — flyout item with two tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="icon_products.png">
        <Tab Title="Active">
            <ShellContent Title="Active"
                          ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ActiveProductsPage}" />
        </Tab>
        <Tab Title="Archived">
            <ShellContent Title="Archived"
                          ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ArchivedProductsPage}" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings — standalone flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="icon_settings.png">
        <ShellContent ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:SettingsPage}" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>
```

**AppShell.xaml.cs**

```csharp
namespace MyApp;

public partial class AppShell : Shell
{
    public AppShell()
    {
        InitializeComponent();

        // Register the detail page so you can navigate with:
        //   await Shell.Current.GoToAsync(
        //       $"{nameof(ProductDetailsPage)}?id={product.Id}");
        Routing.RegisterRoute(nameof(Views.ProductDetailsPage),
                              typeof(Views.ProductDetailsPage));
    }
}
```

**Key points:**

- Each `FlyoutItem` becomes an entry in the flyout menu.
- Wrapping two `Tab` elements inside the Products `FlyoutItem` gives you the bottom tab bar with **Active / Archived**.
- `Routing.RegisterRoute` makes `ProductDetailsPage` a *detail* (push) route — it won't appear in the flyout but can be navigated to programmatically from either product list.
- Use `ContentTemplate` (not `Content`) so pages are created lazily.

To receive the query parameter in `ProductDetailsPage`, add:

```csharp
[QueryProperty(nameof(ProductId), "id")]
public partial class ProductDetailsPage : ContentPage
{
    public string ProductId { get; set; }
    // ...
}
```
